summary: American playwright, most commonly identified by Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? (George, Martha, Nick, Honey, imaginary son) and The Zoo Story (Jerry, Peter, bench in Central Park, poisoned dog, knife impalement). Other notable plays include Seascape, A Delicate Balance, Three Tall Women, The Goat or Who is Sylvia?, The American Dream, and The Sandbox.

works.name: Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?
works.description: Albee's most famous play about the dysfunctional couple George and Martha, who invite the younger couple Nick and Honey over for drinks after a university party. They play cruel games including 'Get the Guests,' 'Hump the Host,' and 'Bringing Up Baby.' George describes a novel about 'Blondie' marrying 'Mousie' with a 'hysterical pregnancy' in the section 'Walpurgisnacht' to humiliate Honey. It is gradually revealed that George and Martha's son is imaginary. Martha quotes Bette Davis's 'what a dump!' Nick teaches biology (Martha thinks math). George tells a story about a boy who shot his mother and ordered a 'bergin and water.'

clues.clue: George and Martha's son is revealed to be imaginary; George says son died in car crash dodging a porcupine
examples: it is slowly revealed that George and Martha's son is imaginary
George and Martha have been lying about the existence of their own son

clues.clue: Games: 'Get the Guests,' 'Hump the Host,' 'Bringing Up Baby'
examples: games like 'Hump the Host' and 'Get the Guests' are played by Nick, Honey, George, and Martha
games like 'Bringing up Baby'

clues.clue: George's 'Get the Guests' novel: blonde professor, 'Mousie' wife, 'hysterical pregnancy' in 'Walpurgisnacht' section
examples: a man describes the plot of a novel about a blonde professor and his wife 'Mousie' who has a 'hysterical pregnancy' in its section 'Walpurgisnacht'

clues.clue: Martha quotes Bette Davis: 'what a dump!'; boy shoots mother, orders 'bergin and water'
examples: A woman quotes Bette Davis's line 'what a dump!' in a play that includes the story of a boy shooting his mother then ordering a 'bergin and water'

clues.clue: Nick teaches biology (Martha thinks math); George claims wife 'eats chromosomes for breakfast'
examples: Nick, teaches this subject, although Martha is convinced he teaches math
his wife 'eats chromosomes for breakfast'

works.name: The Zoo Story
works.description: Albee's first play, a one-act about the encounter between Jerry (a 'permanent transient') and the publishing executive Peter on a bench in Central Park. Jerry describes poisoning his landlady's dog with hamburger meat, describes God as 'a colored queen who wears a kimono,' spells out 'h-o-m-o-s-e-x-u-a-l,' describes an eleven-day relationship with a Greek boy, and asks 'Was trying to feed the dog an act of love?' Jerry pokes Peter, provokes a fight, and ultimately impales himself on a knife held by Peter. Peter repeats 'Oh my god.' Later expanded with the prequel Homelife as At Home at the Zoo.

clues.clue: Jerry impales himself on a knife held by Peter on a bench in Central Park
examples: Jerry impales himself on a knife held by Peter
Jerry forces Peter to take a knife from him, before he impales himself upon it

clues.clue: Jerry poisons landlady's dog with hamburger meat; asks 'Was trying to feed the dog an act of love?'
examples: he used hamburger meat to poison his landlady's dog
his attempts to kill his landlady's dog with poisoned meat

clues.clue: Peter is a publishing executive with wife, two daughters, two parakeets, two cats
examples: A publishing executive has a wife, two daughters, and two parakeets

clues.clue: Jerry describes God as 'a colored queen who wears a kimono and plucks his eyebrows'
examples: God is described as 'a colored queen who wears a kimono and plucks his eyebrows'

clues.clue: Jerry spells out 'h-o-m-o-s-e-x-u-a-l'; eleven-day relationship with Greek boy
examples: describes an eleven-day relationship with a Greek boy when he was 15 and spells out the word homosexual

clues.clue: Homelife prequel added 45 years later; expanded as Edward Albee's At Home at the Zoo
examples: In 2007, Albee wrote this prequel to The Zoo Story that ends with Peter leaving to read at the bench in Central Park

works.name: Seascape
works.description: A play in which the human couple Charlie and Nancy meet the lizard couple Sarah and Leslie on the beach. Punctuated by the sound of offstage jet planes, with discussions about evolution and what a 'brute beast' is. Nancy bares her breasts. Charlie and Nancy think they died from eating expired liver paste sandwiches.

clues.clue: Charlie and Nancy meet lizard couple Sarah and Leslie on beach; discuss evolution
examples: Nancy and Charlie meet the lizard couple Sarah and Leslie on the beach
Nancy and Charlie are visited by a pair of lizards

clues.clue: Punctuated by sound of offstage jet planes; Nancy says 'Such Noise they Make'
examples: punctuated with noises made by an offstage jet
Nancy repeatedly state 'Such Noise they Make'

clues.clue: Charlie explains 'brute beast' and 'flux' as responsible for evolution
examples: a man must explain what a 'brute beast' is to a character who is told that 'flux' is responsible for evolution

clues.clue: Charlie and Nancy think they died from eating expired liver paste
examples: Charlie and Nancy think that they died from eating this expired foodstuff which they put on their sandwiches

works.name: A Delicate Balance
works.description: A play in which Harry and Edna move into the home of their friends Agnes and Tobias after becoming suddenly frightened of something they never explain. Daughter Julia is disturbed to find her room occupied when she comes home after a divorce. Tobias discusses killing a cat that no longer liked him. Tobias delivers an 'aria' yelling 'I don't want you here! I don't love you! But by God... you stay!'

clues.clue: Harry and Edna move into Agnes and Tobias's home after unexplained fright
examples: Harry and Edna take over one of Agnes and Tobias's rooms
Edna and Harry move into their friends house after becoming suddenly frightened

clues.clue: Tobias killed a cat that no longer liked him
examples: Tobias discusses a cat he had killed after he became infuriated by realizing she didn't like him anymore

clues.clue: Tobias's aria: 'I don't want you here! I don't love you! But by God... you stay!'
examples: a man yells 'I don't want you here! I don't love you! But by God... you stay!'

works.name: Three Tall Women
works.description: A play in which the three title characters A, B, and C are revealed to be aspects of the same woman at different ages. In the first act, a 26-year-old lawyer (C) tries to ascertain the correct age of her nonagenarian client (A). A gives a monologue about muscles and sinews of a man she loved.

clues.clue: A, B, and C revealed to be aspects of the same person; 26-year-old lawyer ascertains age of nonagenarian
examples: the title figures, A,B, and C, are revealed to be aspects of the same person
a 26-year-old lawyer tries to ascertain the correct age of her nonagenarian client

works.name: The Goat, or Who is Sylvia?
works.description: Martin, a Pritzker Prize-winning architect, confesses to having a sexual relationship with a goat named Sylvia. The play ends with his wife Stevie dragging the dead goat's carcass onstage. The alternate title references Shakespeare's The Two Gentlemen of Verona. Martin's son Todd discusses a television interview; Martin asks if he's too young for Alzheimer's.

clues.clue: Stevie drags dead goat carcass onstage; Martin is Pritzker Prize-winning architect
examples: Stevie Gray dragging the dead title character back to her husband, a Pritzker Prize-winning architect
Ross watches Stevie drag a carcass onstage

clues.clue: Alternate title references Shakespeare's The Two Gentlemen of Verona ('Who is Silvia?')
examples: The alternate title of The Goat, Who is Sylvia?, refers to this Shakespeare play

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Additional works include The American Dream (Mommy and Daddy mutilate adopted child from Bye-Bye Adoption Service; Grandma packed off), The Sandbox (Angel of Death performs calisthenics on beach), Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-Tung (Minister on ocean liner, Long-Winded Lady), Marriage Play, and Fam and Yam (based on interview with William Inge). Albee owned a cat named Snow.

clues.clue: The American Dream: Mommy and Daddy adopt 'bumble of joy' from Bye-Bye Adoption Service; gouge out eyes, cut off hands; Grandma packed off
examples: Mommy and Daddy mutilated their adopted child after its behavior displeased them
a baby had its eyes gouged out and its hands cut off by Mommy and Daddy

clues.clue: The Sandbox: Angel of Death performs calisthenics on beach; stumbles over lines
examples: the Angel of Death performs calisthenics until approaching a grandmother and stumbling over his lines

clues.clue: Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-Tung: Minister on ocean liner hears Long-Winded Lady and Old Woman
examples: a Minister on an ocean liner listens to various stories from a Long-Winded Lady, an Old Woman, and Chairman Mao Tse-Tung

comprehensive_summary:
Edward Albee is one of the most heavily clued American playwrights in quizbowl, with Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? and The Zoo Story appearing with extreme frequency. Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? centers on the dysfunctional couple George and Martha, who invite the younger couple Nick and Honey for drinks and play cruel games including 'Get the Guests,' 'Hump the Host,' and 'Bringing Up Baby.' The central revelation is that George and Martha's son is imaginary — George claims the son died in a car crash swerving to avoid a porcupine, and Martha shouts 'you cannot do that!' George's 'Get the Guests' game involves describing a novel about 'Blondie' and 'Mousie' in the 'Walpurgisnacht' section. Martha quotes Bette Davis's 'what a dump!' and Nick teaches biology.

The Zoo Story is Albee's first play, depicting Jerry's encounter with the publishing executive Peter on a bench in Central Park. Jerry describes poisoning his landlady's dog with hamburger meat, describes God as 'a colored queen who wears a kimono,' spells out the word 'homosexual,' and ultimately impales himself on a knife held by Peter. The play was expanded 45 years later with the prequel Homelife into Edward Albee's At Home at the Zoo.

Seascape features the human couple Charlie and Nancy meeting the lizard couple Sarah and Leslie on the beach, with offstage jet sounds and discussions of evolution. A Delicate Balance shows Harry and Edna fleeing to Agnes and Tobias's home after an unexplained fright. Three Tall Women reveals A, B, and C as the same woman at different ages. The Goat, or Who is Sylvia? climaxes with Stevie dragging a dead goat onstage. The American Dream features Mommy and Daddy mutilating an adopted child from the Bye-Bye Adoption Service.
